original_hypothesis.title: Missing Whitelist Check in recover() Allows Admin to Inflate asdUSDC Supply
original_hypothesis.hypothesis_description: The `asdUSDC.recover` function allows the owner to mint `asdUSDC` based on the balance of any ERC20 token held by the contract. Because it lacks a whitelist check, an admin (or compromised admin key) can recover a worthless token, minting unbacked `asdUSDC` which can then be used to drain real USDC from the contract.
original_hypothesis.investigation_rationale: Privileged functions must still enforce core protocol invariants. Allowing the admin to bypass the whitelist directly threatens the solvency and 1:1 backing of the stablecoin.
preconditions: Admin calls recover() with a non-whitelisted token address
The contract holds a balance of that token

initial_economic_evidence.suspicious_calculation: Mints asdUSDC based on arbitrary token balance
initial_economic_evidence.manipulation_vector: Admin sends worthless token to contract, calls recover, then withdraws real USDC
initial_economic_evidence.profit_hypothesis: Admin steals 100% of real USDC reserves

economic_questions: Is the admin key secured via multisig/timelock?
validation_requirements: Verify recover() behavior with non-whitelisted tokens

preliminary_attack_economics.potential_profit_range: Up to total USDC TVL in the contract

validation_result.impact_summary: Filtered before validation: attack_realism is LOW
validation_result.detailed_analysis: This hypothesis was auto-rejected because its attack_realism grade is LOW. Preconditions require actions outside the attacker's control (off-chain compromise, victim cooperation, or admin collusion). No LLM validation was performed.
